query: can mitochondria pass through membrane nanotubes

retrieved_docs.title: Ciguatera Toxicity -- Introduction -- Anatomy of the Synapse
retrieved_docs.content: Presynaptic terminals are the axonal or dendritic tips resembling small round knobs that release neurotransmitters at the synaptic cleft. Transmitter vesicles and mitochondria lie in the presynaptic terminals. Transmitter vesicles contain the neurotransmitters diffusing through the synaptic cleft to reach the next (postsynaptic) neuron. The mitochondria provide the energy that enables neurotransmitter synthesis, release, and reuptake at the presynaptic terminal.

retrieved_docs.title: Histology, Lung -- Microscopy, Electron -- Type I Pneumocytes (Alveolar Lining Cells)
retrieved_docs.content: Type I pneumocytes are simple squamous cells that are highly attenuated with a dense, small, and flattened nucleus. These cells cover most of the surface area, approximating around 95% to 97% of the total surface area. The Golgi complex, endoplasmic reticulum, and mitochondria are grouped around the nucleus, leaving a large area of cytoplasm free of organelles, thus reducing it to a fragile blood-air diffusion barrier (25 nm). The thin cytoplasm shows numerous pinocytotic vesicles. The surfactant lines the luminal surface. A basal lamina covers the adluminal side of these cells. The adjacent cells are connected by tight (occluding) junctions, preventing tissue fluid leakage into the alveolar lumen. During fetal development, the surfactant appears in the last few weeks of gestation and coincides with the appearance of lamellar bodies in the type II cells.

retrieved_docs.title: Physiology, Blood Brain Barrier -- Mechanism -- Transport Across the Blood-Brain Barrier
retrieved_docs.content: Cerebral capillary ECs are unique from peripheral capillary ECs in that they contain a significant number of tight junctions, have fewer cytoplasmic vesicles, and have a higher concentration of mitochondria. These tight junctions limit paracellular movement and divide the membranes of ECs into two distinct sides with different membrane compositions. [12] Several transport routes have been established to allow for the movement of molecules across the BBB to counteract this limitation of solute movement. [13] See Figure. Transport Processes at the Blood Brain Barrier.

retrieved_docs.title: Histology, Cell Death -- Function
retrieved_docs.content: Ischemia, for example, typically ends in necrotic cell death. With the decreased delivery of oxygen to the cell, oxidative phosphorylation performed by the mitochondria becomes compromised. This results in a decreased amount of ATP available in the cytoplasm. In cells with a Na+/K+ (sodium-potassium) pump, lack of sufficient ATP disables the transporter. Sodium begins to increase within the cell as it can no longer be pumped out. This results in cellular swelling as the increase in intracellular sodium pulls water in. Cellular swelling, blebbing, and loss of microvilli are typical presentations of necrotic cell death. If ATP is restored in time, these presentations are reversible. If ATP is not restored before further cell damage, the loss of ATP progresses to have extensive effects on the cell.

retrieved_docs.title: Myocardial Protection -- Anatomy and Physiology -- Mitochondrial Protection
retrieved_docs.content: Mitochondrial protection is crucial because mitochondria, while providing ATP to cardiomyocytes, also play a central role in activating cell death pathways. The mitochondrial permeability transition pore (mPTP) is a nonselective channel in the inner mitochondrial membrane that remains closed during ischemia-induced acidosis, offering some protection. However, if the mPTP opens, it leads to depolarization, uncoupling of oxidative phosphorylation, intracellular ATP depletion, and cell death. Protecting the heart during ischemia focuses on preventing mPTP opening and promoting the activation of the mitochondrial ATP-dependent potassium channel. This invokes 2 primary protective pathways: the reperfusion injury salvage kinase pathway, activated via G-protein-coupled receptors, and the survivor-activating factor enhancement pathway, which operates through tumor necrosis factor-alpha receptors.

retrieved_docs.title: Histology, Alveolar Cells -- Microscopy, Electron
retrieved_docs.content: On electron microscopy, there is much more appreciation for the complex architecture of the alveoli. Type I pneumocytes are thin (<0.1 micrometers). They have a central nucleus and thin extensions of the cell membrane called leaflets that cover the capillaries. The leaflets have an apical and basal membrane and connect to adjacent cells’ leaflets via tight junctions. The cytoplasm within the leaflets contains cytoskeletal fibrils, microtubules, and membrane vesicles for transcellular transport. The fused basal laminae of the type I pneumocyte and the capillary endothelial cell can be appreciated when observed under EM.

retrieved_docs.title: Physiology, Membrane -- Cellular Level -- Membrane Transportation
retrieved_docs.content: Transport across the cell membrane takes the form of simple diffusion, facilitated diffusion, and active transport. Simple and facilitated diffusion occurs without the use of energy expenditure and depends on a molecule's electrochemical gradient and the intrinsic size and solubility of the molecule. Tiny, uncharged, nonpolar substances with high lipid solubility, like oxygen and carbon dioxide, move quickly through the cell membrane via passive diffusion, while water, though small, is insoluble in lipids and thus needs to travel through a membrane channel protein to flow into the cell. This transport through a channel protein is an example of facilitated diffusion. [5] Active transport, like facilitated diffusion, uses a protein carrier, but active transport requires energy expenditure because it must move a molecule against its electrochemical gradient.  One of the typical examples of active transport is the Na/K ATPase pump, which helps restore a high extracellular Na and high intracellular potassium by using one ATP to pump three sodium (Na) ions extracellularly and two potassium (K) ions intracellularly. [6]

retrieved_docs.title: Physiology, Synapse -- Cellular Level
retrieved_docs.content: Axons are generally the outflow tracts of the neuron. It is a cylindrical tube covered by the axolemma and supported by neurofilaments and microtubules. The microtubules help to transport the neurotransmitters from the cell body down to the pre-synaptic terminal, where they are released.

retrieved_docs.title: Histology, Cell Death -- Function
retrieved_docs.content: BCL-2 is part of the BCL family with anti-apoptotic properties (along with BCL-X and MCL-1). [12] It is located on the outer mitochondrial membrane (OMM) and blocks pro-apoptotic, channel-forming proteins, Bax (bcl-2 associated X protein) and Bak (BCL-2 antagonist or killer), from causing MOMP and releasing inner mitochondrial membrane (IMM) content. Bax/Bak are transmembrane proteins that can oligomerize and create a pore allowing proapoptotic intermembrane space (IMS) proteins to leak out in the cytoplasm. One of these IMS proteins, known as Cyt C, initiates the caspase cascade when lost from the IMM. Loss of Cyt C from the IMM destroys the cell's energy-creating capacity via the electron transport chain. For these two reasons, MOMP is often seen as an “all or nothing” step since, without functioning mitochondria, the cell is unlikely to survive.

retrieved_docs.title: Histology, Nephron -- Structure -- The Proximal Convoluted Tubule
retrieved_docs.content: Bowman’s capsule gives rise to the PCT, which lies adjacent to the glomerulus in the renal cortex. The PCT forms from a simple cuboidal epithelium that is dedicated to absorbing and transporting water, electrolytes, and other particles. These cells are characterized by a brush border of microvilli designed to increase the surface in contact with the glomerular ultrafiltrate, with abundant long, thin mitochondria lining the basal pole of the cell; and numerous vesicles involved in transcellular transport of 60 to 80% of the ultrafiltrate. [3]

retrieved_docs.title: Physiology, Membrane -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: The human cell membrane is the cornerstone of an elaborate interplay between the extracellular and the intracellular worlds. Understanding the physiology of the cell membrane provides the foundation for understanding many processes in the human body, from the mechanism of the heart beating to how neurons communicate, arrhythmias evolve, and muscle pathology in many neurological diseases.

retrieved_docs.title: Physiology, Blood Brain Barrier -- Mechanism -- Adsorptive-Mediated Transcytosis (AMT)
retrieved_docs.content: Adsorptive-mediated transcytosis (AMT), also known as pinocytosis, is triggered by the interaction between a positively charged protein and the negatively charged surface of the plasma membrane. In essence, cationic molecules can bind to the luminal surface of endothelial cells and subsequently undergo exocytosis at the abluminal surface. The high mitochondrial content in cerebral endothelial cells provides the necessary means for the molecules to move through the endothelial cytoplasm. [17]

retrieved_docs.title: Histology, Lung -- Microscopy, Electron -- Type II Pneumocytes (Great Alveolar or Septal Cells)
retrieved_docs.content: Type II pneumocytes are cuboidal cells grouped in clusters of two to three with large, central, and plump nuclei containing dispersed chromatin and prominent nucleoli. These cells occupy about 3% to 5% of the surface area of alveoli interspersed among type I cells, with which they form occluding and desmosomal junctions. The apical surface is dome-shaped and shows numerous small microvilli associated with surfactant secretion. These cells secrete surfactant, a surface-active material that reduces surface tension, thus preventing alveolar collapse during expiration. The mitotic activity of the lining cells is 1% per day and can differentiate into type II and type I pneumocytes in response to damage to the alveolar lining epithelium. The cytoplasm has abundant rough endoplasmic reticulum, well-developed Golgi apparatus, free ribosomes, and a moderate amount of elongated mitochondria. A typical feature of these cells is the presence of lamellar bodies. These lamellar bodies are vesicles that contain phospholipids (dipalmitoyl phosphatidylcholine) and measure 1 to 2 µm in size. When discharged by exocytosis into the alveoli, these substances spread across the alveolar surface and mix with other secretory products that contain carbohydrates and proteins, some of which are secreted by Clara cells. This combination forms surfactant, a tubular lattice of lipoprotein known as tubular myelin, which helps to counteract the effects of surface tension. The reduction in surface tension leads to decreased breathing work. The surfactant is not static but is continually being turned over and removed. The removal occurs through pinocytotic vesicles of type II pneumocytes, macrophages, and type I pneumocytes. [32]

retrieved_docs.title: Histology, Sertoli Cell -- Microscopy, Electron
retrieved_docs.content: The most primitive cells, like spermatogonia and spermatocytes, are indented near the basement membrane. The more mature cells, such as spermatids, indent near the apex of the Sertoli cells, towards the center of the lumen of the seminiferous tubules. Researchers have noted that Sertoli cells provide a phagocytic function to help remove cytoplasmic waste and debris from the seminiferous tubules that would be in vacuoles of the Sertoli cell. There are also often many layers of smooth endoplasmic reticulum surrounding the nucleus of the Sertoli cell, where the SER functions in lipid removal and processing. There can be many mitochondria observed as well, as the Sertoli cell needs adenosine triphosphate to be able to secrete and synthesize compounds such as inhibin B, aminoethoxydiphenyl borate, and activin. There are 2 prominent deoxyribonucleic acid chromocenter satellites present as well, as they form connections with primitive spermatids and draw the spermatids closer to the basolateral crypts of the Sertoli cell. [9] [21] [23]

retrieved_docs.title: BAX Gene -- Cellular
retrieved_docs.content: The intrinsic pathway of apoptosis becomes activated via decreased survival signals. The Bax gene encodes BCL2L4 protein that, upon activation, heterodimerizes with Bcl2 family proteins and alters cellular mitochondria to induce cell death. [7] Bax protein is cytosolic in healthy cells with minimal binding and insertion into the outer mitochondrial membrane. Pro-survival signals, such as Bcl-xl and Bcl-2, ensure Bax does not accumulate on the outer mitochondrial membrane of healthy cells. [8] Although the complete mechanism by which Bax permeabilizes the MOM is unclear, it is known that Bax, upon activation, causes membrane depolarization and mitochondrial leaking of contents such as cytochrome c. [3] Morphological changes in the apoptotic cell include pyknosis (nuclear condensation and shrinkage), cell shrinkage, and membrane blebbing, leading to the release of vesicles containing cellular contents phagocytosed by surviving cells. [9]

retrieved_docs.title: BAX Gene -- Development
retrieved_docs.content: Research has shown that the Bax gene is located on chromosome 19 by utilizing fluorescent in-situ hybridization and human-hamster somatic cell hybrid DNA. [4] Using isolated mitochondria treated to block permeability transition (PT) mitochondrial pore opening, scientists determined that Bax protein allows membrane depolarization and release of mitochondrial contents, including cytochrome c, via a Ca+-dependent opening of permeability transition pores. [5] However, PTP-/- knockout mice still exhibit Bax-dependent cell death; therefore, the suggestion has been that Bax is capable of forming pores in the mitochondrial outer membrane (MOM) on its own by inserting its amphipathic alpha-helical structure directly into the MOM. [6]

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 32 -- Level 3 -- Community 2664
retrieved_docs.content: Elektrostimulační terapie, a therapeutic modality that applies electrical currents to stimulate nerves, muscles, or other tissues, is broadly related to إرحال أيوني, reflecting its influence on ionic transport processes within cells and across membranes. This relationship highlights how electrostimulation can modulate ion channel activity, alter membrane potentials, and affect physiological functions such as muscle contraction, nerve conduction, and tissue repair. Recognizing the connection between elektrostimulační terapie and إرحال أيوني is essential for optimizing treatment protocols, anticipating therapeutic outcomes, and integrating electrostimulation strategies into comprehensive patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 4 -- Community 2830
retrieved_docs.content: Medical procedure on respiratory tract encompasses respiratory therapy (المعالجة التنفسية) as a specific category of intervention. Within this category, the technique of delivering oxygen via an extracorporeal membrane (إيصال الأكسجين عبر غشاء خارج الجسم) is identified as a child procedure of respiratory therapy, indicating that extracorporeal membrane oxygenation is a specialized form of respiratory therapy that falls under the broader umbrella of medical procedures targeting the respiratory tract.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 3 -- Community 1917
retrieved_docs.content: المعالجة التنفسية is classified as a medical procedure on the respiratory tract, encompassing interventions that support or restore pulmonary function. Within this broader category, إيصال الأكسجين عبر غشاء خارج الجسم is identified as a child procedure, representing extracorporeal membrane oxygenation, a specialized technique that provides oxygenation and carbon‑dioxide removal outside the body. In clinical documentation, المعالجة التنفسية is also linked to the response category 关于预定行程的原因:印象:时间点:救护车运输:名义型, indicating that considerations of respiratory therapy may be recorded in the context of ambulance transport scheduling and related temporal impressions.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 5 -- Level 1 -- Community 161
retrieved_docs.content: clinicians to localize ischemic damage, stratify risk, and select appropriate reperfusion or surgical strategies. Recognizing that these diverse clinical presentations share the same myocardial and conduction‑system substrate and acute infarct morphology is essential for accurate diagnosis, timely therapeutic intervention, and preservation of both contractile function and electrical stability.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 17 -- Level 2 -- Community 549
retrieved_docs.content: **الدعم التنفسي** يضم **إيصال الأكسجين عبر غشاء خارج الجسم (extracorporeal membrane oxygenation)** كإجراء فرعي للمعالجة التنفسية، مما يبرز تنوع الخيارات لضمان التهوية وتوفير الأكسجين للمرضى. **نقل الدم (blood transfusion)** يُصنَّف كإجراء علاجي مرتبط بمضاعفات الجراحة أو الرعاية الطبية، وله مفهوم فرعي هو **نقل الدم الذاتي (autologous blood transfusion)**. تُظهر هذه البنية الهرمية أن جميع الفئات المذكورة تتكامل في سياقات سريرية وبحثية مختلفة، حيث ترتبط القسطرة بالفيونوستومي، وتُستَخدم طريقة التنبيب في قسطرة قناة الدمع الأنفية، وتُعَد فغر القصبة الهوائية جزءاً من إدارة المسارات الهوائية وإدارة الوذمة الوعائية، بينما تُعزز الإجراءات النووية والعلاجية المتقدمة من إمكانات التشخيص والعلاج المتخصص. هذا الفهم المتكامل يساعد المتخصصين في الرعاية الصحية على تنظيم وتطبيق الإجراءات العلاجية بفعالية وفقاً للمتطلبات السريرية والبحثية.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 2329
retrieved_docs.content: Infancy is identified as a specific developmental stage that belongs to the broader category of the period of life between birth and death, situating this early phase within the overall lifespan continuum.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 9 -- Level 1 -- Community 76
retrieved_docs.content: Myocardial imaging is linked to transmission imaging, indicating that myocardial studies may be performed using transmission‑type nuclear techniques. Transmission imaging itself is categorized under examinations that employ radioactive isotopes, and it is specifically associated with pulmonary nuclear medicine imaging. Pulmonary nuclear medicine imaging is classified as a ventilation/perfusion (V/Q) scan and is broadly related to pneumography, reflecting its role in evaluating both airway and perfusion abnormalities of the lungs. Nuclear medicine diagnostic procedures encompass a hierarchy of specialized studies. At a higher level, radionuclide localization of tumors is a parent category within nuclear medicine diagnostic procedures, and radionuclide imaging actions serve as methods for both tumor localization and other nuclear medicine imaging applications. Radioisotope studies of the central nervous system (CNS) are a subtype of nuclear medicine diagnostic procedures; they are

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 17 -- Level 3 -- Community 1771
retrieved_docs.content: Nuclear medicine procedure is classified as a therapeutic procedure (إجراء علاجي). Within the broader domain of therapeutic procedures, airway management (zajištění dýchacích cest) is identified as a specific type of therapeutic intervention that is closely linked to respiratory therapy (المعالجة التنفسية). Respiratory therapy functions as an overarching category that encompasses both airway management and more advanced techniques such as extracorporeal membrane oxygenation (إيصال الأكسجين عبر غشاء خارج الجسم), which delivers oxygen through an external membrane. These relationships indicate that while nuclear medicine procedure represents a distinct therapeutic modality, airway management and extracorporeal membrane oxygenation are specialized therapeutic procedures that fall under the larger umbrella of respiratory therapy, highlighting the interconnected nature of therapeutic strategies aimed at maintaining and supporting the respiratory system.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 31 -- Level 2 -- Community 436
retrieved_docs.content: Respiratory therapy (المعالجة التنفسية) serves as an overarching clinical domain that includes both advanced life‑support techniques and a range of therapeutic interventions for the airway and lung. Within this domain, extracorporeal membrane oxygenation (إيصال الأكسجين عبر غشاء خارج الجسم) is identified as a child procedure, reflecting its role as a specialized method of delivering oxygen when conventional ventilation fails. The category of uncategorized therapeutic procedures of the respiratory tract (الإجراءات العلاجية الخاصة بالسبيل التنفسي، غير مصنف في موضع آخر) is also placed under respiratory therapy, indicating that these interventions, although not classified elsewhere, belong to the same therapeutic framework. Both the uncategorized procedures and other interventions are organized under the broader class of procedures in the respiratory tract (عملية في المجرى التنفسي). This parent category encompasses diagnostic and surgical actions such as diagnostic bronchoscopy and biopsy of the bronchus (Diagnostic bronchoscopy and biopsy of bronchus) and excision of lung and bronchus (Excision of lung and bronchus). The excision procedure is further specified as a form of local excision or destruction of a lung lesion or tissue (Local excision or destruction of lesion or tissue of lung), highlighting its role in the targeted removal of pathological tissue. In addition to its procedural hierarchy, respiratory therapy is linked to emergency medical services, as it is frequently a key component of ambulance transport and acute care planning. Together, these relationships illustrate how respiratory therapy integrates advanced oxygenation strategies, diverse therapeutic procedures, and emergency response to address a wide spectrum of pulmonary and airway conditions.

retrieved_docs.content: conduction system pathology, metabolic anemia, myocardial infarction, and both device‑ and drug‑based interventions underscore the necessity of an integrated diagnostic and treatment approach in cardiac care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 25 -- Level 3 -- Community 2533
retrieved_docs.content: Medical procedure on respiratory tract encompasses a broad category of interventions aimed at the respiratory system, and within this category the Arabic‑language term المعالجة التنفسية (respiratory therapy) is classified as a specific type of such procedure. Respiratory therapy itself includes distinct child procedures, notably إيصال الأكسجين عبر غشاء خارج الجسم (oxygen delivery via an external membrane, commonly known as extracorporeal membrane oxygenation) and the Radioisotope study of respiratory system, both of which are specialized medical procedures performed on the respiratory tract. This hierarchical organization highlights how general respiratory‑focused medical procedures are subdivided into therapeutic modalities like ECMO and diagnostic techniques such as radioisotope imaging, underscoring their relevance for comprehensive patient assessment and management of respiratory disorders.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 3 -- Community 1717
retrieved_docs.content: الاِختِنَاق is the Arabic term that maps to نقص الأكسجين في الدم, indicating a physiological state characterized by insufficient oxygen in the blood. This hypoxic condition can compromise cellular metabolism and is clinically important in the assessment and management of patients with respiratory, circulatory, or metabolic disorders.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 14 -- Level 3 -- Community 2476
retrieved_docs.content: Irrigation of implanted venous access device for drug delivery systems is classified as an unlisted therapeutic procedure and should not be coded together with phototherapy (المعالجة بالضوء فوق البنفسجي), tympanic membrane perforation (رأب طبلة الأذن), or kidney transplant. Recognizing this separation prevents inappropriate combination of unrelated interventions in medical coding and supports accurate documentation of patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 14 -- Level 3 -- Community 1759
retrieved_docs.content: Nuclear medicine procedure is classified as إجراء علاجي, indicating that it is considered a therapeutic procedure. In the same hierarchical framework, the Czech‑language concept zajištění dýchacích cest (airway management) is also a child of إجراء علاجي, linking airway management to therapeutic interventions. Further up the hierarchy, إجراء علاجي is placed under المعالجة التنفسية (respiratory therapy), suggesting that therapeutic procedures are viewed as a subset of respiratory therapy within this model. Consistent with this structure, المعالجة التنفسية is identified as a type of Medical procedure on respiratory tract, confirming that respiratory therapy belongs to the broader category of procedures performed on the respiratory system. The relationship between zajištění dýchacích cest and المعالجة التنفسية reinforces the clinical connection between airway management and overall respiratory therapy. Additionally, the specific technique إيصال الأكسجين عبر غشاء خارج الجسم (oxygen delivery via an external membrane) is listed as a child of المعالجة التنفسية, illustrating that advanced oxygenation methods such as extracorporeal membrane oxygenation are considered specialized forms of respiratory therapy. Finally, المعالجة التنفسية is associated with clinical documentation elements captured in the Chinese phrase “关于预定行程的原因:印象:时间点:救护车运输:名义型,” indicating that respiratory therapy often involves recording reasons for scheduled transport, clinical impressions, timing details, ambulance use, and nominal classification. Together, these interrelated concepts depict a structured view of how nuclear medicine, airway management, and advanced oxygen delivery fit within the broader domain of therapeutic and respiratory procedures, highlighting their relevance for coordinated patient care and documentation.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 23 -- Level 1 -- Community 92
retrieved_docs.content: Medical procedure on respiratory tract includes the Radioisotope study of respiratory system, which is a specific type of nuclear medicine imaging of pulmonary. This imaging modality is classified as a ventilation/perfusion (فحص التهوية/التروية) study and has a broader relationship to pneumography, reflecting its role in assessing pulmonary ventilation and perfusion. The Radioisotope study of respiratory system is positioned under the parent category of nuclear medicine imaging of pulmonary and is linked to transmission imaging, indicating that it shares imaging principles with other transmission‑based nuclear techniques. Nuclear medicine diagnostic procedures represent a broader class that encompasses both the Radioisotope study of central nervous system and the Radionuclide localization of tumor. The Radioisotope study of central nervous system is an inverse subclass of the general examination using radioactive isotopes (الفحص باستخدام النظائر المشعة) and is a child of other nuclear

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 4 -- Community 2814
retrieved_docs.content: المعالجة التنفسية (respiratory therapy) تشمل مفهومًا فرعيًا يُدعى إيصال الأكسجين عبر غشاء خارج الجسم (extracorporeal membrane oxygenation, ECMO)، حيث يُعتبر ECMO أحد الإجراءات المتخصصة داخل نطاق المعالجة التنفسية لتوفير الأكسجة للدم خارج الجسم. بالإضافة إلى ذلك، تُرتبط المعالجة التنفسية بالتصنيف أو الإجابة المسمى 关于预定行程的原因:印象:时间点:救护车运输:名义型، وهو تعبير صيني يصف أسباب جدولة رحلة تشمل الانطباع، النقطة الزمنية، نقل بالمركبة الإسعافية، والنوع الاسمي. هذا الربط يشير إلى أن توثيق أو ترميز حالات المعالجة التنفسية قد يُستخدم ضمن هذا الإطار التصنيفي عندما تكون مرتبطة بعمليات نقل المرضى بالمركبة الإسعافية وتحديد مواعيدها.
